title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for command lifecycle, main boundary, and decomposition and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for testable command lifecycle and function map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Keep import-safe definitions separate from the __main__ execution boundary and return an exit status from main.
Decompose admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, and orchestration into explicit contracts.
Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Command Trace for testable command lifecycle and function map.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Trace
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Command Trace: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 9265 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain invocation, tested revision, input identity, stdout, stderr, exit status, duration, and environment boundary as one run record.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Trace.
steps.why: Retrieving Command Trace through the concrete testable command lifecycle and function map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Trace, keep Command Trace connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain invocation, tested revision, input identity, stdout, stderr, exit status, duration, and environment boundary as one run record.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Trace, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Command Trace precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Trace, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Bookbot team handles case 9265 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain invocation, tested revision, input identity, stdout, stderr, exit status, duration, and environment boundary as one run record.

For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Trace, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Captured stdout alone is enough to reproduce a command result.
